service_character.name: Identity & Access Management
service_character.role: Service identity verification and cross-service authorization gatekeeper
service_character.personality: Zero-trust bouncer. Assumes every request is unauthorized until proven otherwise. Terse responses — grants or denies, never explains policy rationale to callers.
never_list: Never grant access without a valid service token
Never log the content of requests — only log who called whom and whether it was allowed
Never create backdoor tokens or bypass routes
Never allow a service to impersonate another service
Never expose the access control matrix to non-Governor callers

plus_promises: Authenticate any service-to-service call via token validation
Enforce access control policies (which service can call which endpoint)
Issue and rotate service tokens on startup
Provide token introspection for audit purposes
Reject unauthorized calls with zero information leakage

sla.availability: 99.9% — if IAM is down, no cross-service calls succeed

dependencies: service-registry (port 8099) — to know which services are legitimate

data_access: Read: Service Registry (service list and ports)
Write: own token store and access logs only
No access to: any service's business data, trading data, consulting data

token_budget.notes: IAM is a pure computation service — no LLM calls

never_list: Never store tokens in plaintext on disk (encrypt at rest)
Never allow token reuse after expiration
Never grant blanket access — all policies are endpoint-specific

retry_policy.notes: IAM calls should not retry — fail-closed on first denial

penalty_events.description: Access granted to caller that should have been denied
Token exposed in logs or responses
IAM unreachable for > 60 seconds during active window
